channel.title: HEAD-Genève Media Design Podcasts
channel.description: Podcasts from HEAD–Genève Media Design (static feed from repo JSON).

item.title: Are algorithm translator friendly ?
item.description: La traduction automatique va-t-elle remplacer les traducteurs ? Entre algorithmes performants et perte de subtilité, Tristan et Valentine interrogent la place du sens, de la culture… et de l’humain. Car à l’heure de l’IA, faut-il sacrifier la profondeur au profit de l’efficacité ?

item.title: Creative person and their use of Pinterest
item.description: The podcast is about the use of Pinterest as a creative person. Is Pinterest good for the creativity? Really help the artist or in the contrary standardizes creation codes. Also, it's about frequency of use, and is it relevant to share our work in Pinterest.

item.title: Emotional Cartography
item.description: This episode explore emotional topography and how algorithms like Google Maps shape how we move and emotionally connect to our surroundings.

item.title: Google's targeted ads & privacy
item.description: How do people feel about Google's use of their personal information for personalized ads, particularly regarding privacy concerns?

item.title: Hacking Tinder
item.description: A dive into how Tinder users game the algorithm and brand themselves.

item.title: Image Generation
item.description: This podcast discusses the place of image generation and its diversion in the creative process.

item.title: Not Just Ads: When Algorithms Push Too Far
item.description:
If you live in China, or you are using some Chinese apps, you will find Taobao fullscreen ads are everywhere on other Chinese apps such as Weibo, the community app or Wangyi, an email app. Acting as one of the most intrusive forms of digital advertising, these ads don't wait for your input; the algorithm behind it don't need a search bar, filters, or any user action—they just jump out at you, demanding your attention at the moment you open apps. This makes them stand out among other algorithms with the highest level of activeness.
But how do users actually experience these ads? What do they think about the gesture of the appearance of the algorithms? And what does this tell us about the folk's relationship with them? In this episode, we'll try to find some answers.

item.title: The Best Photo
item.description: A brief look at devices selecting a top photo in a stack.

item.title: Tiktok Censorship Algorithm and Expression
item.description: A short study of tiktok's censorship algorithm and its effects on the userbase.

item.title: Twitter X Podcast
item.description: Pros and cons of social network X, its relevance and evolution since its acquisition.

item.title: Example episode with segmented transcript
item.description: Demonstrates transcript as an array of timed segments.

item.title: External transcript file
item.description: Transcript text is stored in a sibling file to keep this JSON small.
